tokens.range: to have affection for (sexually or otherwise)
Jehovah, Jewish national name of God
Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch
a residence (including a shepherd's hut, the lair of animals, figuratively, the grave; also the Temple); specifically, the Tabernacle (properly, its wooden walls)
Tsijon (as a permanent capital), a mountain of Jerusalem
an opening, i.e. door or gate

flags.note: יהוה — The divine name (YHWH). LORD (small caps) hides the personal name. Yahweh/Jehovah are reconstructions of the consonants. The MKT's choice shapes the reader's sense of intimacy with God.

2.struct: contains a main verb; ⚑ theologically contested vocabulary

tokens.range: a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term (and in such cases frequently not expressed in translation)
to say (used with great latitude)
to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage

properly, to be erect (i.e. stand perpendicular); hence (causatively) to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal (establish, fix, prepare, apply), or figurative (appoint, render su
an elevation, i.e. (adj.) lofty (compar.); as title, the Supreme
Tsijon (as a permanent capital), a mountain of Jerusalem

gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used (in the plural thus, especially with the article) of the supreme God; occasionally applied by way of deference to magistrates; and sometimes as a supe
perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively (of words), to speak; rarely (in a destructive sense) to subdue
to be heavy, i.e. in a bad sense (burdensome, severe, dull) or in a good sense (numerous, rich, honorable; causatively, to make weighty (in the same two senses)
suspension (of music), i.e. pause
a city (a place guarded by waking or a watch) in the widest sense (even of a mere encampment or post)

flags.note: אֱלֹהִים — Grammatically plural, normally takes singular verbs when referring to Israel's God. Translating as "God" (singular) interprets the plural; "gods" would be technically accurate but theologically wrong in monotheistic contexts.

to grave, by implication, to write (describe, inscribe, prescribe, subscribe)
